name: step-by-step
description: /step-by-step (alias /sbs) — plan-first sequential runbook. Decomposes the task into an ordered, numbered plan, then executes every step strictly in order WITHOUT pausing between steps, narrating each step's start + ✅/⚠/❌ result. Halts only on a step failure (reports the failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) or before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). The deliberate single-threaded counterpart to /cycle's parallel fan-out — one thread, top to bottom. Bare = the task in context; /step-by-step <task> targets explicitly.
